2606.22474.title: Not All Claims Are Equally Risky: FACTOR for Adaptive Verification in Factual Long-Form Generation
2606.22474.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) generate fluent long-form text, however, often add unsupported factual claims. Existing verification techniques improve factuality by grounding generation in external evidence. However, the same verification policy usually applies to all claims despite being differences in hallucination risks. We propose \textit{FACTOR} (\textit{FACTuality-Oriented Risk-aware Verification}), an inference-time model that adapts verification criteria according to claim-level uncertainty. FACTOR combines uncertainty estimation, adaptive language inference verification, and candidate re-ranking to allocate verification effort where it is most needed. We evaluate \textit{FACTOR} on FactScore benchmark showing that adaptive verification improves factuality while reducing verification cost simultaneously. We further perform different ablation studies to identify the primary driver of these gains. Our results show the effective and model-agnostic performance of \textit{FACTOR} for improving factuality in long-form generation.
2606.22474.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）生成流暢的長篇文本，但經常添加不支持的事實聲明。現有的驗證技術通過將生成基於外部證據來提高事實性。然而，通常對所有聲明應用相同的驗證政策，儘管幻覺風險存在差異。我們提出了 \textit{FACTOR}（\textit{FACTuality-Oriented Risk-aware Verification}），這是一種推理時模型，根據聲明級別的不確定性調整驗證標準。FACTOR 結合了不確定性估計、自適應語言推理驗證和候選者重新排序，以將驗證工作分配到最需要的地方。我們在 FactScore 基準上評估 \textit{FACTOR}，顯示自適應驗證在同時降低驗證成本的同時提高了事實性。我們進一步進行了不同的消融研究，以確定這些增益的主要驅動因素。我們的結果顯示 \textit{FACTOR} 在提高長篇生成的事實性方面具有有效且模型無關的性能。
2606.22474.author: Areeba Hassan et.al.
2606.22474.authors: Areeba Hassan, Arooj Kausar, Syeda Kisaa Fatima, Gibrail Islam, Mehwish Fatima
